mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS held near the zero lower bound (0.13%), continued low policy rates keep the equity risk premium compressed relative to historical norms, supporting a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as no hiking cycle has begun.
regime_hint: zero lower bound / pre-liftoff monetary regime
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero rate periods are not statistically higher than during rate-hiking periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 5.5% and continues declining, the labor market approaches full employment, raising market expectations of imminent Fed tightening; this triggers a defensive repricing that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month as investors discount future rate hikes.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.5% and falling for 2+ consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening ahead of policy normalization
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the sign of the average return during trigger periods flips across two consecutive rolling 2-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) signals broad-based consumer demand strength that is not decomposable from either series alone; when both accelerate together, forward-looking earnings expectations rise, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. Neither series alone reliably predicts returns without the other confirming demand breadth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 0%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample shows no statistically significant return premium versus single-series triggers (RSAFS-only or HOUST-only) at p > 0.10, indicating the interaction adds no incremental signal.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion regime where INDPRO growth decelerates from prior peaks while remaining positive, the slowdown in industrial output signals waning supply-side momentum that precedes broader economic deceleration, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as earnings growth expectations are revised down.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating industrial output
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following INDPRO deceleration periods are not significantly lower than the unconditional historical mean (p > 0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing event where FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% for the first time since the zero-lower-bound period began signals the start of policy normalization, causing an abrupt short-term repricing of equity valuations and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets adjust discount rate assumptions.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% (first hike off zero lower bound)
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX return following the liftoff crossing is not negative on average across the first three instances observed, or is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects continued labor market expansion that supports consumer income and spending, which feeds into corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month, regardless of the broader macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the correlation reverses across two consecutive 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of moderate but positive GDPC1 growth (below 2.5% annualized) combined with rising RSAFS, the economy is judged to be in a stable mid-cycle expansion, and forward S&P 500 returns over the next quarter are positive as earnings growth remains supported without triggering overheating fears. In a high-growth regime above 3.5%, the same combination instead signals overheating risk and the relationship flips to negative.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth between 1.5% and 2.5% with RSAFS growth positive
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns in the moderate-growth regime are not significantly different from returns in the high-growth regime (p > 0.10), indicating the regime split adds no explanatory power.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between low CPIAUCSL inflation growth and near-zero FEDFUNDS produces a persistently negative real policy rate; when this combination holds, real yields remain suppressed, favoring equity valuations over bonds and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. The effect requires both low inflation and low nominal rates jointly, since either alone (e.g., low rates with rising inflation) would not sustain negative real rates.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during joint low-inflation/low-rate periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05), or the effect disappears when tested with either series alone at similar thresholds.
